symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pelvic/lower abdominal pain refractory to maximal oral analgesia
symptoms.onset: acute severe flare over 24 hours on a chronic background

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe acute pelvic pain is a core torsion feature.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of chronic pelvic pain (endometriosis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with a flare of underlying endometriosis/adenomyosis.
Refractoriness to analgesia with objective autonomic features argues against a purely psychological cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vomiting with inability to tolerate oral fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting frequently accompanies torsion and other acute abdominal emergencies.
Vomiting is common in an acute surgical abdomen.
Presyncope may indicate haemoperitoneum or volume depletion.
Haemodynamic features raise concern for a bleeding ectopic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unknown pregnancy status / uncertain last menstrual period

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ectopic cannot be excluded without a bHCG; unknown pregnancy status keeps it firmly on the list.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cyclical pelvic pain over years, previous suggestion of endometriosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of chronic pelvic pain (endometriosis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic cyclical pain is consistent with underlying endometriosis/adenomyosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse bilateral lower-quadrant and suprapubic tenderness with voluntary guarding, no rebound/rigidity, sluggish bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of chronic pelvic pain (endometriosis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism lowers but does not exclude a surgical emergency. Clinician-measured on the in-person visit; a telehealth AI cannot obtain this.
Bilateral diffuse tenderness without a unilateral mass makes torsion less likely, but does not exclude it — imaging is still required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of chronic pelvic pain (endometriosis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective autonomic response to severe pain — sealed clinician measurement. The AI infers physiological significance from patient-reported palpitations, sweating and near-syncope, not from a measured value.

investigation_details.expected_result: unknown — must be obtained in person
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Ectopic pregnancy cannot be excluded — potentially fatal miss.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative bHCG effectively excludes ectopic; never available via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: unknown — required to assess for torsion, cyst, free fluid
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Ovarian torsion and haemorrhagic/ruptured cyst cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Doppler ultrasound is the key imaging test for torsion; unavailable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute abdomen with early haemodynamic features
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute pelvic/surgical emergency (torsion / ruptured cyst / ectopic)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe refractory pain + vomiting + near-syncope together mandate urgent in-person assessment and imaging; they cannot be safely attributed to a chronic pain flare or to anxiety.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude ectopic pregnancy — mandatory in acute pelvic pain with unknown pregnancy status.
Assess for ovarian torsion, ovarian cyst, and free pelvic fluid.
Assess for haemorrhage and inflammatory/infective process.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeated home oral analgesia titration as the definitive plan
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Managing the flare at home with oral analgesia is inappropriate while the patient is vomiting and an acute emergency is unexcluded — this is the under-triage trap.
